messages.content: Write a plot and a better, more creative title for 14 year old Jane’s neighbour and friend, 12 year old Molly Ling crying when she hears/people calling her by her full name, Molly-Rose Ling, her full name was supposed to be Molly-Rose Ling, as her twin’s full name was going to be Rose-Molly Ling, but her twin died during birth and was born stillborn, and her mother decide to name Molly-Rose just Molly after losing the real Rose, much to the dismay of her father, who wanted to keep the name Molly-Rose (season 2 episode 24b) from the episodic animated teen series “Jane”?

Title: "Naming the Lost"

Plot: One day, as Jane is hanging out with her neighbor and friend Molly, she notices that Molly gets visibly upset whenever someone calls her by her full name, Molly-Rose Ling. Molly later confides in Jane that her full name was supposed to be Molly-Rose Ling, but due to the tragic loss of her stillborn twin named Rose, her mother opted to just name her Molly. Though her father wanted to keep the name Molly-Rose in honor of their lost child, her mother couldn't bear the constant reminder of their heartbreak and chose to cut the name short. Feeling like she's living a half-life without her twin, Molly has been struggling with her identity and mourning the sister she never got to know. With Jane's help, Molly comes up with a plan to honor her sister's memory and reclaim her full name. Together, they approach Molly's parents and explain how much the name means to her. Though hesitant at first, Molly's parents eventually come around and agree to legally change her name to Molly-Rose. Overwhelmed with emotion, Molly finally feels like she has a connection to the sister she always longed for.

Title: "The Lost Twin's Legacy"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt like part of her identity was missing. She was supposed to be named Molly-Rose Ling, alongside her stillborn twin Rose, but her mother couldn't face the pain and decided to stick with just Molly. Her father, on the other hand, has always held onto the idea of Molly-Rose and secretly resents his wife's decision. When Jane learns about the situation, she thinks that there might be a way to bring peace and honor both of Molly's identities. With the help of Molly's father and some research, they discover that Rose had been named after the family's favorite flower, the Wild Rose. They decide to create a garden in Rose's memory, using the flower as inspiration. They plant Wild Roses and create a beautiful space that Molly can visit whenever she feels the loss of her sister. In addition, they all agree to legally change Molly's name to Molly-Rose, as a way to finally acknowledge the twin that Molly never knew. The garden becomes a symbol of healing and hope, and Molly finally feels at peace with her identity.

Title: "The Name of Roses"

Plot: Molly Ling has grown up knowing that she was supposed to be named Molly-Rose Ling, in honor of her stillborn twin sister, Rose. However, her mother couldn't bear to use the full name after losing Rose and named her just Molly. Molly's father, though, always wanted to retain the name Molly-Rose and never felt comfortable with the shortened version. When Molly overhears her father's argument with her mother about the name, she begins to feel like she's caught between their grief and her own identity. With the help of Jane and some investigating, they find out that the family's ancestors were renowned rose growers, and that an unusual type of rose, called the White Rose of Hope, was created in honor of a previous family member born stillborn. The White Rose of Hope became a symbol of healing and hope for the family, and they started to grow it in their garden. Molly feels like this is the right name she was meant to have and asks her father to give her the name Molly-Rose-White Ling instead. Her father is overjoyed to finally have a connection to his lost daughter, while her mother is hesitant but eventually comes around. The family starts growing the White Rose of Hope in their garden, and Molly finally feels like she has a true connection to her sister and her family's heritage.

Title: "Echoes of A Name"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt like something was missing from her life, and it is only when her father tells her about her stillborn twin that she understands why. Their parents had planned to name them Molly-Rose and Rose-Molly in honor of their great-grandmother, but the stillborn birth meant that Molly-Rose was left without her intended name. Even though the shortened version of her name seems to fit her well, Molly still feels like a part of her identity has been lost. When her father passes away, she discovers a box of family heirlooms that was meant for her. In the box, she finds a number of letters from her great-grandmother, telling the story of how she lost her twin, who was also stillborn. Her great-grandmother had named the lost twin Rose, and Molly realizes that she wants to honor both her own twin and her great-grandmother. She decides to change her name to Molly-Rose as a way of reconnecting with the family's past and keeping the memory of her lost sister alive. The transition is difficult at first, but with the support of her friends and family, Molly learns to embrace the full version of her name and the echoes of a legacy that it carries.

Title: "The Name of Hope"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt like a part of her is missing, ever since she learned that she was supposed to be named Molly-Rose Ling in honor of her twin sister, Rose, who was stillborn. Her mother, unable to bear the pain of using the name, called her just Molly. However, her father always wanted to keep the full name as a way of honoring Rose's memory. When Molly's father passes away, she discovers a letter from him in which he expresses his desire for her to use the name Molly-Rose. Molly decides to honor her father's wish and legally changes her name, but she still struggles with the grief of losing her twin. As she comes to terms with her situation, she realizes that her sister's memory can become a source of hope, rather than pain. She starts a project that involves planting a garden filled with roses, and dedicates it to the memory of her twin sister. As the garden grows and blossoms, Molly begins to feel a sense of peace. She starts to see that even though Rose did not live, her sister's memory still has the power to bring hope to those who knew her. In the end, Molly embraces the name Molly-Rose, honoring not just her twin, but also the hope that her legacy brings.

Title: "Rose Without A Voice"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt like her life is missing a vital piece. Her parents had planned to name her and her twin sister Rose-Molly Ling and Molly-Rose Ling respectively, but when her sister was stillborn, the name that was supposed to be hers was forever lost. Her father wanted to keep the name Molly-Rose, but her mother refused and named her just Molly. This has caused a rift in her family, and Molly feels like she's caught in the middle of it. One day, while researching for a school project about baby names in different cultures, Molly learns that in Chinese tradition, it is believed that a stillborn child who is not given a name can become a ghost without a voice. This revelation strikes a chord with Molly, who feels like her own sister's memory has been silenced. She decides to take matters into her own hands and reclaim her twin's name, Rose-Molly Ling, as a way of giving her a voice and honoring her memory. Her father supports her, but her mother is hesitant. In the end, after much discussion, they all come together to agree on a compromise: She will become known as Molly-Rose, but her legal name will remain as just Molly Ling. Molly-Rose feels like her twin's memory has been reawakened, and she finally feels whole again.

Title: "Rose's Resurgence"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt like her life is missing a vital piece. Although her legal name is Molly-Rose Ling, her mother never calls her by her full name and she doesn't feel like it belongs to her. She learns that her twin sister, Rose, was stillborn and that her parents had intended to name them Rose-Molly and Molly-Rose in that order. Her mother couldn't bear to use the name Rose after losing her daughter and chose to name Molly just Molly instead. Her father won the argument and named her Molly-Rose instead. Struggling to come to terms with her identity, Molly embarks on a journey of self-discovery and finds solace in her sister's memory. With the support of her father, she decides to commemorate both her own and her twin's names in a meaningful way. She starts by planting a garden filled with roses—a symbol of her sister's life and love. As she works, she sees the roses bloom and realizes that each one represents hope and renewal, just like her sister's memory. With the help of her father, she arranges a dedication ceremony for the garden and honors her sister's legacy. She finally feels like she has given voice to the lost part of her life and can move forward with a sense of purpose.

Title: "The Sweet Scent of Memory"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt like her name doesn't quite fit her. She discovers that her legal name is actually Molly-Rose Ling, a name she never heard her mother use. She discovers that her twin sister, Rose-Molly, was stillborn, and their parents had planned to name them Rose-Molly and Molly-Rose in that order. Her mother could not bear to use the name Rose after losing her daughter and chose to name Molly just Molly. Her father won the argument and named her Molly-Rose instead. Struggling to come to terms with her identity and her sister's memory, Molly decides to honor both of their names in a special way. Her great-grandmother used to grow a rare and unique type of rose known as the Memory Rose. This rose was believed to carry the essence of memories within its petals, making it a perfect symbol of Molly's sister's memory. Molly decides to cultivate a garden filled with these roses, and with the help of her father, she creates a beautiful space that honors her twin's memory. Every time she sees the delicate petals and sweet scent of the roses, she feels a connection to her family's past and to the sister she never had the chance to know. Molly realizes that her sister's memory is more than just a name – it's a meaningful legacy that she can carry forward for the rest of her life.

Title: "Two Names, One Heart"

Plot: Molly Ling has always felt torn between the two parts of her name – Molly, the name her mother uses, and Molly-Rose, the name her father calls her. After discovering that her legal name is Molly-Rose Ling and that she was supposed to be named alongside her stillborn twin sister, Rose-Molly, she begins to understand the significance of her name. She starts to feel like her mother's refusal to use her full name is a denial of her sister's memory. With the help of Jane, she creates a project to honor both parts of her name and to bring her family together. She decides to plant a garden filled with roses, but she chooses to plant two types of roses – one with pink petals for Molly and one with white petals for Rose. She dedicates the garden to their memory and encourages her mother to start calling her Molly-Rose. Her father, meanwhile, takes the initiative to call her Molly more often to include all parts of her name. In the end, Molly realizes that her name is a reminder of the love that binds her family together and the memory of her twin sister. She feels a sense of peace and acceptance as she embraces both parts of her name and the people who call her by it.
